config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar yang dilindungi, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa batas, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang lemah atau minoritas. Saya setuju kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan membatasi hak individu hanya sejauh benar-benar perlu dan adil. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak per orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically outrank the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to excuse government overreach and abuse of minority rights. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and limits on state power. If “society” becomes a blank check, then almost any policy can be justified by claiming it helps the majority. In my view, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only limits them when there is a clear, narrow, and necessary reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, menormalkan ketidakadilan, atau membungkam kritik, padahal justru hak individu yang kuat membuat masyarakat lebih sehat. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi hanya sah jika pembatasan hak itu jelas, perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, istilah “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi kedok untuk kekuasaan yang sewenang-wenang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea makes me wary because it can easily become a justification for censorship, discrimination, or unchecked government power, especially when the “common good” is defined by whoever is in charge. That said, I am not arguing for absolute individualism; public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require limits, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and subject to strong legal review. To me, the burden should always be on society to prove why a right must be restricted, not on the individual to justify keeping it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu gampang dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, diskriminasi, atau keputusan yang menguntungkan kelompok kuat, jadi saya tidak mau memberi blank cheque ke “masyarakat” atau negara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, terutama untuk kesehatan publik dan ketertiban, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa diuji secara adil. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir dari perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, bukan dari pengorbanan hak yang dibungkus bahasa kebersamaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. In the United States, we have seen too many cases where “the common good” was used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, so I am skeptical of any claim that asks people to trust society first and rights later. That does not mean individual rights are absolute in every situation, but any restriction has to be narrow, necessary, and tightly checked by law. If society can override rights whenever it wants, then minority protections become fragile and the majority can always claim moral cover.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
